suitable_climate: Warm and humid climate, temperature between 20°C to 35°C. High rainfall (100-150 cm) or assured irrigation.
suitable_soil: Clayey loam or clay soils that can retain moisture. Ideal pH range is 5.5 to 7.0.
land_preparation: Ploughing 3-4 times, followed by puddling (wet land preparation) to a depth of 10-15 cm to create an impermeable layer that holds water.
seed_selection: Select certified, high-yielding, disease-resistant varieties suitable for your region (e.g., Mansuli, Radha-4, Pokhareli Jethobudo in Nepal). Conduct salt-water test to remove lightweight/floating seeds.
seed_rate: 40 to 50 kg per hectare (approximately 2 to 2.5 kg per ropani).
nursery_or_direct_sowing: Nursery transplanting is preferred. Sow seeds in wet nursery beds, grow seedlings for 21-25 days before transplanting.
sowing_time: May to June for monsoon paddy; December to January for spring paddy (Chaite).
planting_steps: Transplant 2-3 healthy seedlings (21-25 days old) per hill at a depth of 2-3 cm. Maintain shallow water level (2-3 cm) during transplanting.
irrigation_schedule: Maintain 5 cm of standing water during vegetative and flowering stages. Drain water completely 10-15 days before harvesting.
manure_and_fertilizer: Apply 10-15 tons of well-decomposed farmyard manure (FYM) or compost per hectare. Recommended N:P:K is 100:60:40 kg/ha. Note: Exact quantity depends on soil test, climate, variety, and local agriculture office advice.
organic_fertilizer_option: Apply 10-12 tons of compost, 2 tons of vermicompost, and green manure (like Sesbania/Dhanicha) incorporated during land preparation.
chemical_fertilizer_option: Apply Urea (217 kg/ha), Single Super Phosphate (375 kg/ha), and Muriate of Potash (67 kg/ha).
fertilizer_schedule_by_stage: Basal dose: 50% nitrogen, 100% phosphorus, and 100% potassium at the time of final land preparation. Tillering stage: 25% nitrogen top dressing (25-30 days after transplanting). Panicle initiation stage: 25% nitrogen top dressing (50-60 days after transplanting).
weed_management: Hand weeding twice at 20-25 days and 40-45 days after transplanting, or apply pre-emergence herbicides like Butachlor 3-4 days after transplanting.
pest_management: Control Stem Borer and Leaf Folder using Trichogramma cards (biological option) or Cartap Hydrochloride (chemical option). Control Stem Borer with light traps.
disease_management: Prevent Blast and Bacterial Leaf Blight by seed treatment with Agrosan. Spray Tricyclazole for Blast if symptoms appear.
best_production_tips: Implement System of Rice Intensification (SRI) which uses single young seedlings, wide spacing, and alternate wetting/drying to increase yield by 30-50%.
harvesting_stage: Harvest when 80-85% of the grains in the panicle turn golden-yellow and moisture content drops to around 20-22%.
harvesting_method: Cut the straw close to the ground using sickles or a combine harvester. Bind into sheaves and dry for 2-3 days.
post_harvest_handling: Thresh immediately after drying. Winnow to clean the grains, and dry them until moisture content is reduced to 12-14% for safe storage.
expected_yield: 3.5 to 5.5 tons per hectare (175 to 275 kg per ropani) depending on variety and management.
common_mistakes: Transplanting over-aged seedlings (older than 30 days) reduces tillering and yield. Keeping excessive water throughout the crop cycle without drainage reduces soil aeration.
safety_note: Ensure proper protective gear (mask, gloves) when handling chemical fertilizers or spraying pesticides. Keep all agro-chemicals out of reach of children and animals.

suitable_climate: Warm weather crop, grows best in temperatures between 21°C to 27°C. Needs moderate rainfall (50-80 cm). Highly sensitive to waterlogging.
suitable_soil: Deep, fertile, well-drained loamy to silty soils rich in organic matter. Ideal pH range is 6.0 to 7.5.
land_preparation: Ploughing 2-3 times to a depth of 20 cm, followed by harrowing to achieve a fine tilth. Ensure proper drainage channels are made.
seed_selection: Choose certified high-yielding varieties or hybrids (e.g., Rampur Composite, Deuti, or Rajkumar hybrid in Nepal) adapted to your altitude.
seed_rate: 20 to 25 kg per hectare (approximately 1 kg per ropani).
nursery_or_direct_sowing: Direct sowing in lines is recommended.
sowing_time: March to April for hill maize; April to May for summer maize; October to November for winter maize in Terai.
planting_steps: Sow seeds at a depth of 3-5 cm. Place one seed per hill. Firm the soil lightly over the seeds to ensure good contact.
irrigation_schedule: Water the crop at critical stages: seedling stage, knee-high stage, tasseling, silking, and grain filling. Avoid standing water.
manure_and_fertilizer: Apply 10-12 tons of compost/FYM per hectare. Recommended N:P:K is 120:60:40 kg/ha. Note: Exact quantity depends on soil test, climate, variety, and local agriculture office advice.
organic_fertilizer_option: Apply 10-15 tons of compost, 1.5 tons of neem cake, and biofertilizers like Azotobacter during sowing.
chemical_fertilizer_option: Apply Urea (260 kg/ha), Single Super Phosphate (375 kg/ha), and Muriate of Potash (67 kg/ha).
fertilizer_schedule_by_stage: Basal dose: Apply 33% nitrogen, 100% phosphorus, and 100% potassium at planting time. Knee-high stage: Top-dress 33% nitrogen. Tasseling/Silking stage: Top-dress remaining 34% nitrogen.
weed_management: Interculture and earthing up at 25-30 days and 50-55 days after sowing. Apply Atrazine herbicide as pre-emergence.
pest_management: Monitor Fall Armyworm (FAW) regularly. Use Neem-based pesticides or biological control with Bacillus thuringiensis. In severe cases, spray Spinetoram.
disease_management: Treat seeds to prevent Turcicum Leaf Blight and Downy Mildew. Spray Metalaxyl if Downy Mildew is observed.
best_production_tips: Earthing up (hilling) at knee-high stage is crucial to prevent lodging and promote strong brace roots.
harvesting_stage: Harvest when the husks turn papery-dry and grains become hard with a black layer at the base (physiological maturity).
harvesting_method: De-husk the ears directly from standing plants or harvest the entire stalk, pile them up, and then de-husk.
post_harvest_handling: Dry de-husked cobs in the sun for 4-5 days. Shell the grains and dry them to 12% moisture level before storing in airtight bags.
expected_yield: 3.0 to 5.0 tons per hectare (150 to 250 kg per ropani) for composites; up to 7-9 tons/ha for hybrids.
common_mistakes: Sowing seeds too deep (>5 cm) causes poor germination. Over-watering and lack of drainage kills maize plants due to root rot.
safety_note: Wear protective masks when handling seed treatments or spraying chemical pesticides. Dispose of empty containers safely.

suitable_climate: Cool and dry winter climate. Ideal temperature during growing is 15°C to 22°C. Requires moderate moisture.
suitable_soil: Well-drained clay loam or sandy loam soils. pH range of 6.0 to 7.5.
land_preparation: Ploughing 3-4 times followed by disc harrowing and planking to create a very fine, level seedbed.
seed_selection: Use high-yielding, rust-resistant certified varieties (e.g., NL-971, Bhrikuti, Gautam, or Vijay in Nepal).
seed_rate: 120 kg per hectare (approximately 6 kg per ropani).
nursery_or_direct_sowing: Direct sowing by drilling or broadcasting.
sowing_time: November to December (optimal is mid-November).
spacing: 20-22 cm row-to-row spacing, seeds placed continuously in lines.
planting_steps: Sow seeds in rows at a depth of 4-5 cm. Planking should be done after broadcasting/sowing to cover seeds.
irrigation_schedule: Critical irrigation stages: Crown Root Initiation (CRI) at 20-25 days, Tillering stage (40-45 days), Late Jointing (60-65 days), Flowering (80-85 days), and Milking stage (100-105 days).
manure_and_fertilizer: Apply 6-10 tons of FYM/compost per hectare. Recommended N:P:K is 100:50:50 kg/ha. Note: Exact quantity depends on soil test and local advice.
organic_fertilizer_option: Incorporate green manure during monsoon, apply 10 tons of well-rotted manure and Azotobacter biofertilizer.
chemical_fertilizer_option: Apply Urea (217 kg/ha), Single Super Phosphate (312 kg/ha), and Muriate of Potash (83 kg/ha).
fertilizer_schedule_by_stage: Basal dose: Apply 50% nitrogen, 100% phosphorus, and 100% potassium at sowing. First top dressing: 25% nitrogen at CRI stage (first irrigation). Second top dressing: 25% nitrogen at jointing stage (second irrigation).
weed_management: Hand weeding or weeding with a wheel hoe at 30 days. Apply Sulfosulfuron or Metribuzin to control broadleaf weeds and wild oats.
pest_management: Monitor for Aphids. Spray Neem oil or biological formulations. Use yellow sticky cards to monitor population.
disease_management: Grow resistant varieties to avoid Leaf Rust and Yellow Rust. Seed treatment with Vitavax to prevent Loose Smut.
best_production_tips: Never skip the first irrigation at the Crown Root Initiation (CRI) stage (20-25 days after sowing); it is the most critical yield-determining factor.
harvesting_stage: Harvest when straw and ears turn golden yellow, grains become hard, and moisture drops to 15%.
harvesting_method: Cut manually with sickles or use a power reaper/combine harvester.
post_harvest_handling: Thresh the harvested bundles, dry the wheat grains in the sun for 3-4 days to reduce moisture to 12% for safe storage.
expected_yield: 3.0 to 4.5 tons per hectare (150 to 225 kg per ropani).
common_mistakes: Sowing late (after mid-December) drastically reduces yield due to hot winds during grain filling. Skipping irrigation at CRI stage.
safety_note: Ensure safety when operating threshing machinery. Avoid inhaling grain dust during threshing; wear a protective mask.

suitable_climate: Cool climate crop. Daytime temperature of 18°C-24°C and night temperature of 10°C-15°C is ideal for tuberization.
suitable_soil: Loose, well-drained sandy loam or alluvial soils rich in organic matter. Avoid clay. pH of 5.2 to 6.5.
land_preparation: Deep ploughing up to 25 cm followed by 2-3 harrowings. Soil must be loose and free of clods to allow proper tuber expansion.
seed_selection: Use certified disease-free, well-sprouted seed tubers of suitable varieties (e.g., Cardinal, Janakdev, Kufri Jyoti in Nepal). Tubers should weigh 30-50 grams.
seed_rate: 1500 to 2000 kg of seed tubers per hectare (approximately 75 to 100 kg per ropani).
nursery_or_direct_sowing: Direct planting of sprouted tubers.
sowing_time: September to October for plains (Terai); January to February for mid-hills; March to April for high hills.
planting_steps: Open furrows, place sprouted seed tubers with sprouts pointing upwards at a depth of 5-8 cm. Cover with soil, creating a ridge.
irrigation_schedule: Moderate but frequent irrigation. Keep ridges moist but not waterlogged. Water at stolon formation (25-30 days) and tuber growth (45-60 days). Stop watering 10 days before harvesting.
manure_and_fertilizer: Apply 20-30 tons of compost/FYM per hectare. Recommended N:P:K is 120:80:100 kg/ha. Note: Exact quantity depends on soil test and local advice.
organic_fertilizer_option: Apply 25 tons of compost, 2 tons of mustard cake, and 2.5 tons of chicken manure during land preparation.
chemical_fertilizer_option: Apply Urea (260 kg/ha), Single Super Phosphate (500 kg/ha), and Muriate of Potash (167 kg/ha).
fertilizer_schedule_by_stage: Basal: Apply 50% nitrogen, 100% phosphorus, and 100% potassium in furrows before planting. Top dressing: 50% nitrogen during earthing up (30-35 days after planting, when plants are 15-20 cm high).
weed_management: Earthing up at 30-35 days is critical. It covers growing tubers and eliminates weeds.
pest_management: Monitor Potato Tuber Moth (PTM) and Aphids. Use pheromone traps for PTM. Spray neem extract for Aphids.
disease_management: Watch out for Late Blight (devastating). Spray Mancozeb or Metalaxyl preemptively if cool, cloudy, and humid weather occurs.
best_production_tips: Dehaulming (cutting the green vines) 10-12 days before harvesting helps in skin hardening of tubers, reducing post-harvest damage.
harvesting_stage: Harvest when leaves turn yellow and dry. Skin of the tubers should be firm and not peel off easily when rubbed.
harvesting_method: Carefully dig out tubers using a spade or potato digger, avoiding cuts.
post_harvest_handling: Cure tubers in a shaded, well-ventilated room for 7-10 days to heal skin wounds. Sort out damaged/diseased ones, store in cool, dry, dark place or cold storage.
expected_yield: 15 to 25 tons per hectare (750 to 1250 kg per ropani).
common_mistakes: Earthing up poorly, leaving growing tubers exposed to sunlight, which turns them green and toxic (solanine formation).
safety_note: Do not consume green potatoes as they contain toxic solanine. Wash hands thoroughly after handling treated seed tubers.

suitable_climate: Warm-season crop, grows best in temperatures between 21°C to 24°C. Sensitive to frost and heavy waterlogging.
suitable_soil: Well-drained sandy loam or clay loam soils rich in organic matter. pH of 6.0 to 7.0.
land_preparation: Ploughing 3-4 times, adding compost, and creating raised beds (1 meter wide) to ensure excellent drainage.
seed_selection: Select high-yielding or hybrid seeds (e.g., Srijana, Avinash, or Roma in Nepal). Hybrid seeds yield higher and are disease-resistant.
seed_rate: 150 to 200 grams of hybrid seeds per hectare (8-10 grams per ropani).
nursery_or_direct_sowing: Nursery sowing in raised beds. Transplant seedlings after 4-5 weeks (when they have 4-5 leaves).
sowing_time: September to October for winter crop; January to February for spring crop; June to July for rainy season crop.
planting_steps: Transplant single healthy seedling per hill on raised beds. Water immediately. Plant during late afternoon to reduce transplant shock.
irrigation_schedule: Provide regular and uniform watering. Avoid dry spells followed by heavy watering as it causes fruit cracking.
manure_and_fertilizer: Apply 15-20 tons of compost/FYM per hectare. Recommended N:P:K is 120:80:80 kg/ha. Note: Exact quantity depends on soil test.
organic_fertilizer_option: Apply 20 tons of compost, vermicompost (3 tons), and bone meal. Use organic liquid manure (Jholmol) weekly.
chemical_fertilizer_option: Apply Urea (260 kg/ha), Single Super Phosphate (500 kg/ha), and Muriate of Potash (133 kg/ha).
fertilizer_schedule_by_stage: Basal: 33% nitrogen, 100% phosphorus, and 100% potassium at transplanting. Top dressings: 33% nitrogen at 30 days (first flowering) and 34% nitrogen at 60 days (fruit setting).
weed_management: Shallow hoeing and weeding twice at 20 and 40 days. Mulching with straw helps control weeds and conserve moisture.
pest_management: Watch out for Fruit Borer. Use pheromone traps and spray Bacillus thuringiensis or Spinosad for chemical control.
disease_management: Late Blight and Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl virus. Spray Copper Oxychloride for fungal diseases; control whiteflies to prevent viral spread.
best_production_tips: Staking (supporting plants with bamboo sticks and ropes) keeps fruits off the ground, reducing rot and improving quality.
harvesting_stage: Harvest at different stages depending on market distance: green wrap (for long-distance shipping), pink/turning (local markets), or red-ripe (immediate use/processing).
harvesting_method: Pluck fruits gently with a twisting motion, retaining the calyx.
post_harvest_handling: Sort fruits by size and color. Remove cracked/diseased ones. Pack in plastic crates and store in cool, shaded areas.
expected_yield: 25 to 40 tons per hectare (1250 to 2000 kg per ropani) for open varieties; up to 60-80 tons/ha for hybrids under greenhouse.
common_mistakes: Irregular watering causing Blossom End Rot (black spots at the bottom of fruits due to calcium deficiency) and fruit cracking.
safety_note: Always wash harvested tomatoes thoroughly before consumption. Follow withholding periods after spraying any pesticide.

suitable_climate: Cool and moist climate. Optimum temperature is 15°C to 20°C. High temperature during curding causes leafy and yellow curds.
suitable_soil: Deep, rich, well-drained loamy soil with high organic matter. pH of 6.0 to 7.0.
land_preparation: Plough 3-4 times. Apply 20 tons of FYM. Make raised beds to avoid water stagnation.
seed_selection: Choose varieties based on maturity: Early (e.g., Silver Cup, Kathmandu Local), Mid-season (e.g., Snowball, Snow Crown).
seed_rate: 400 to 500 grams per hectare (20-25 grams per ropani).
nursery_or_direct_sowing: Sow in nursery beds under net. Transplant seedlings when 4 weeks old.
sowing_time: May to June (early varieties); July to August (mid varieties); September to October (late varieties).
planting_steps: Transplant seedlings in the evening. Press the soil firmly around roots and water immediately.
irrigation_schedule: Requires constant soil moisture. Irrigate every 7-10 days. Do not allow water to stand around the stems.
manure_and_fertilizer: Apply 20 tons of compost. Recommended N:P:K is 120:80:80 kg/ha. Boron (15 kg/ha Borax) is highly recommended. Note: Exact quantity depends on soil test.
organic_fertilizer_option: Apply 25 tons of compost, vermicompost, and organic boron sources. Apply neem cake.
chemical_fertilizer_option: Apply Urea (260 kg/ha), Single Super Phosphate (500 kg/ha), Muriate of Potash (133 kg/ha), and Borax (15 kg/ha).
fertilizer_schedule_by_stage: Basal: 50% nitrogen, 100% phosphorus, 100% potassium, and Borax at transplanting. Top dressing: 25% nitrogen at 30 days and 25% at 50 days (before curd initiation).
weed_management: Interculture operation (hoeing and weeding) twice at 25 and 45 days. Keep soil loose.
pest_management: Diamondback Moth (DBM) is the major pest. Spray Bacillus thuringiensis or Cartap Hydrochloride.
disease_management: Clubroot and Downy Mildew. Rotate crops to avoid clubroot; apply Dithane M-45 for Downy Mildew.
best_production_tips: Blanching (covering the white curd with outer leaves and tying them) protects the curd from direct sun, preserving its white color.
harvesting_stage: Harvest when curds reach full size, are compact, firm, and white, before they start turning loose or yellow.
harvesting_method: Cut the curd with a sharp knife, retaining a few jacket leaves to protect curds during transit.
post_harvest_handling: Keep curds in shade. Pack in ventilated crates. Transport during cool hours.
expected_yield: 15 to 25 tons per hectare (750 to 1250 kg per ropani).
common_mistakes: Skipping Boron application leads to hollow stems and brown spots on the curd (browning). Delaying harvest makes curds ricey/hairy.
safety_note: Avoid using heavy chemical sprays close to harvesting time. Ensure proper washing of curds.

suitable_climate: Cool and moist climate. Temperature range of 15°C to 21°C is ideal. Can tolerate moderate frost.
suitable_soil: Fertile, well-drained sandy loam to clay loam soils. pH of 6.0 to 6.8.
land_preparation: Plough 3 times to get a fine tilth. Incorporate 20 tons of compost. Build drainage channels.
seed_selection: Use varieties like Green Coronet, Pride of India, or Golden Acre.
seed_rate: 400 to 500 grams per hectare (20-25 grams per ropani).
nursery_or_direct_sowing: Nursery transplanting. Sow in nursery beds, transplant 25-30 days old seedlings.
sowing_time: August to September for winter crop; January to February for spring crop.
planting_steps: Plant seedlings at a depth similar to nursery bed. Irrigate immediately after transplanting.
irrigation_schedule: Irrigate every 8-10 days during dry spells. Maintain uniform moisture. Reduce watering when heads are fully formed to prevent splitting.
manure_and_fertilizer: Apply 15-20 tons of compost. Recommended N:P:K is 120:60:60 kg/ha. Note: Exact quantity depends on soil test.
organic_fertilizer_option: Apply 20 tons of FYM/compost, poultry manure (1.5 tons), and ash. Weekly application of compost tea.
chemical_fertilizer_option: Apply Urea (260 kg/ha), Single Super Phosphate (375 kg/ha), and Muriate of Potash (100 kg/ha).
fertilizer_schedule_by_stage: Basal: 50% nitrogen, 100% phosphorus, and 100% potassium at transplanting. Top dressing: 25% nitrogen at 30 days and 25% at 50 days (head formation stage).
weed_management: 2-3 hand weedings and hoeing before head formation to keep soil loose and aerated.
pest_management: Diamondback moth, Aphids, and Cabbage butterfly. Use yellow sticky cards, neem oil, or Cypermethrin for chemical control.
disease_management: Black rot and Clubroot. Treat seeds with hot water before sowing; drench soil with copper fungicides for black rot.
best_production_tips: Do not irrigate excessively when heads are mature, otherwise the heads will split and lose market value.
harvesting_stage: Harvest when heads are firm, compact, and reach full typical variety size.
harvesting_method: Cut the head at the base with a sharp knife, leaving 2-3 wrapper leaves for protection.
post_harvest_handling: Remove damaged outer leaves. Pack in crates and keep in a cool, shaded environment.
expected_yield: 20 to 35 tons per hectare (1000 to 1750 kg per ropani).
common_mistakes: Over-fertilizing with nitrogen causes loose, puffy heads and makes the plant susceptible to pests.
safety_note: Wear gloves while harvesting. Observe local pesticide spray safety guidelines and pre-harvest intervals.

suitable_climate: Mild climate without extreme heat or cold. Temperature of 15°C to 25°C is ideal. Requires dry weather during bulb maturity.
suitable_soil: Sandy loam to clay loam soils with high organic matter, excellent drainage, and no stones. pH of 6.0 to 7.0.
land_preparation: Plough 4 times, break clods, level the field, and divide into small flat beds with irrigation channels.
seed_selection: Select varieties like N-53, Agrifound Dark Red, or Nasik Red.
seed_rate: 8 to 10 kg per hectare (400-500 grams per ropani).
nursery_or_direct_sowing: Sow in nursery beds, transplant seedlings when 6-7 weeks old (15 cm tall).
sowing_time: October to November in hills and plains.
planting_steps: Transplant seedlings at a shallow depth of 2-3 cm. Do not plant too deep, as it restricts bulb expansion. Water immediately.
irrigation_schedule: Irrigate every 7-10 days during vegetative growth. Stop irrigation 15 days before harvesting to allow drying and bulb curing.
manure_and_fertilizer: Apply 15-20 tons of compost/FYM per hectare. Recommended N:P:K is 80:50:80 kg/ha. Note: Exact quantity depends on soil test.
organic_fertilizer_option: Apply compost (20 tons), poultry manure (2 tons), and wood ash (rich in potash).
chemical_fertilizer_option: Apply Urea (174 kg/ha), Single Super Phosphate (312 kg/ha), and Muriate of Potash (133 kg/ha).
fertilizer_schedule_by_stage: Basal: 50% nitrogen, 100% phosphorus, and 100% potassium at transplanting. Top dressing: 25% nitrogen at 30 days and 25% nitrogen at 45 days after transplanting.
weed_management: Onion is a close-spaced crop and competes poorly with weeds. Hand weed 2-3 times. Herbicide Pendimethalin can be used.
pest_management: Onion Thrips are the major pest. Spray Neem oil or Spinosad. Use blue sticky traps.
disease_management: Purple Blotch and Downy Mildew. Spray Mancozeb or Copper Oxychloride for Purple Blotch.
best_production_tips: Curing (drying bulbs in shade for 7-10 days after harvest until necks are dry) is essential to prevent rot during storage.
harvesting_stage: Harvest when 50-70% of the plant tops fall over (neck fall stage).
harvesting_method: Pull out bulbs manually. Leave them in the field for 2-3 days for field curing if the weather is dry.
post_harvest_handling: Cut tops leaving 2.5 cm neck. Sort bulbs, reject diseased ones, store in ventilated crates or hanging racks in a cool, dry room.
expected_yield: 15 to 25 tons per hectare (750 to 1250 kg per ropani).
common_mistakes: planting seedlings too deep, which leads to elongated, thin bulbs. Watering up to the harvest day, causing bulb rot in storage.
safety_note: Store bulbs in dry conditions. Wet conditions cause mold and rot which can produce toxic mycotoxins.

suitable_climate: Cool season crop. Requires cool and moist weather during vegetative growth and dry warm weather for bulb maturity.
suitable_soil: Rich, well-drained loamy soil with high organic matter. Avoid heavy clay. pH of 6.0 to 7.0.
land_preparation: Plough 3-4 times. Level the land. Prepare flat beds with proper drainage channels.
seed_selection: Use large, healthy, disease-free cloves separated from garlic bulbs. Varieties: Local Garlic, Chinese Garlic.
seed_rate: 500 to 700 kg of cloves per hectare (25-35 kg per ropani).
nursery_or_direct_sowing: Direct planting of cloves.
sowing_time: September to November.
planting_steps: Plant cloves vertically with the pointed end facing upwards at a depth of 3-5 cm. Press soil lightly.
irrigation_schedule: Irrigate immediately after planting, then every 10-14 days. Stop watering 3 weeks before harvesting when leaves start drying.
manure_and_fertilizer: Apply 15-20 tons of compost. Recommended N:P:K is 100:50:50 kg/ha. Note: Exact quantity depends on soil test.
organic_fertilizer_option: Incorporate 20 tons of FYM, wood ash, and vermicompost. Mulch with dry leaves after planting.
chemical_fertilizer_option: Apply Urea (217 kg/ha), Single Super Phosphate (312 kg/ha), and Muriate of Potash (83 kg/ha).
fertilizer_schedule_by_stage: Basal: 50% nitrogen, 100% phosphorus, and 100% potassium at planting. Top dressing: 25% nitrogen at 30 days and 25% nitrogen at 60 days after planting.
weed_management: Manual hand weeding twice at 30 and 60 days. Mulching with straw is highly recommended to suppress weeds.
pest_management: Thrips and Nematodes. Use neem cake in soil for nematodes; spray neem oil for thrips.
disease_management: Purple Blotch and Bulb Rot. Treat cloves with Carbendazim before planting. Avoid waterlogging.
best_production_tips: Mulch the beds with straw or dry leaves immediately after planting cloves to conserve moisture and control weeds.
harvesting_stage: Harvest when leaves turn yellow-brown and 50% of the outer leaves dry up.
harvesting_method: Carefully dig out the bulbs with a fork or hand hoe without damaging the outer skin.
post_harvest_handling: Tie bulbs in bundles and dry in shade for 10-15 days. Store in cool, well-ventilated dry rooms on racks.
expected_yield: 8 to 12 tons per hectare (400 to 600 kg per ropani).
common_mistakes: Planting cloves upside down, which causes twisted, weak plants and poor bulbs. Harvesting late, causing bulb wrapping to split.
safety_note: Handle cloves carefully to avoid bruising. Avoid skin contact with concentrated garlic extracts used for organic pest sprays.

suitable_climate: Warm and humid climate. Grows well from sea level up to 1500m altitude. Needs partial shade and high rainfall.
suitable_soil: Sandy loam, clay loam, or red lateritic soil with high organic matter, excellent drainage, and aeration. pH of 6.0 to 6.5.
land_preparation: Plough deep 3-4 times. Prepare raised beds (1 meter wide, 15-20 cm high) of required length to prevent water logging.
seed_selection: Use healthy, disease-free seed rhizomes (e.g., Nase, Kapurkot-1 in Nepal) weighing 30-40 grams, each having 1-2 active buds.
seed_rate: 1500 to 2000 kg of rhizomes per hectare (75-100 kg per ropani).
nursery_or_direct_sowing: Direct planting of rhizomes.
sowing_time: April to May (before monsoon).
planting_steps: Make 5 cm deep pits on raised beds, place rhizome pieces flat with buds pointing upwards, cover with soil and mulch immediately.
irrigation_schedule: Needs constant moisture. Irrigate every 10-14 days during dry periods. Waterlogging is fatal and causes rhizome rot.
manure_and_fertilizer: Highly nutrient-demanding. Apply 25-30 tons of compost. Recommended N:P:K is 75:50:50 kg/ha. Note: Exact quantity depends on soil test.
organic_fertilizer_option: Apply 30 tons of compost, poultry manure, mustard cake, and heavy leaf mulching (three times: at planting, 40 days, and 90 days).
chemical_fertilizer_option: Apply Urea (163 kg/ha), Single Super Phosphate (312 kg/ha), and Muriate of Potash (83 kg/ha).
fertilizer_schedule_by_stage: Basal: Apply 100% phosphorus and 50% potassium at planting. Top dressing: Apply 50% nitrogen + 50% potassium at 40 days; apply remaining 50% nitrogen at 90 days (during earthing up).
weed_management: Hand weeding at 40 and 90 days, followed by immediate mulching and earthing up to cover growing rhizomes.
pest_management: Rhizome Fly and Shoot Borer. Drench soil with neem decoction. Clean field sanitation is key.
disease_management: Rhizome Rot (highly destructive). Treat seed rhizomes with Mancozeb + Carbendazim solution before planting. Ensure excellent drainage.
best_production_tips: Heavy mulching with green leaves (15-20 tons/ha) at planting is critical to preserve soil moisture, organic matter, and suppress weeds.
harvesting_stage: Harvest at 8 months for dry ginger (when leaves turn yellow and dry) or 5-6 months for green ginger.
harvesting_method: Carefully dig out the rhizome clumps with a spade or digging fork, clean the dirt.
post_harvest_handling: Wash rhizomes, remove roots, dry in sun for 1 day. For dry ginger, peel the outer skin carefully and dry in sun for 7-10 days.
expected_yield: 15 to 20 tons per hectare (750 to 1000 kg per ropani) of fresh ginger.
common_mistakes: planting without seed treatment, which leads to total crop failure from Rhizome Rot. Poor drainage causing rotting of rhizomes.
safety_note: When peeling ginger for processing, wear protective masks to avoid irritation from dust and strong ginger odors.

suitable_climate: Hot and humid climate with temperatures between 20°C to 30°C. Requires abundant rainfall or regular irrigation.
suitable_soil: Well-drained, loose sandy loam or clay loam soils rich in humus. pH of 6.0 to 7.5.
land_preparation: Plough 4 times, prepare raised beds 1m wide, 15-20cm high to ensure no water stagnation.
seed_selection: Use healthy, disease-free mother or finger rhizomes (e.g., Kapurkot Haldi-1 in Nepal) with 1-2 eyes.
seed_rate: 1500 to 2000 kg of rhizomes per hectare (75-100 kg per ropani).
nursery_or_direct_sowing: Direct sowing of rhizomes.
sowing_time: April to May.
planting_steps: Place rhizomes flat in furrows/pits at 5 cm depth. Cover with soil, press lightly, and apply heavy leaf mulch.
irrigation_schedule: Keep soil moist. Irrigate every 10-15 days. Avoid standing water.
manure_and_fertilizer: Apply 20-25 tons of compost. Recommended N:P:K is 90:60:60 kg/ha. Note: Exact quantity depends on soil test.
organic_fertilizer_option: Apply 25 tons of compost, poultry manure, vermicompost, and heavy green leaf mulch at planting and top dressings.
chemical_fertilizer_option: Apply Urea (195 kg/ha), Single Super Phosphate (375 kg/ha), and Muriate of Potash (100 kg/ha).
fertilizer_schedule_by_stage: Basal: 100% phosphorus and 50% potassium at planting. Top dressing: 50% nitrogen + 50% potassium at 40 days; 50% nitrogen at 80 days during weeding and earthing up.
weed_management: Weeding at 40 and 80 days. Follow with earthing up and re-applying mulch.
pest_management: Shoot Borer and Rhizome Scale. Clean cultivation, spray neem formulations.
disease_management: Rhizome Rot and Leaf Spot. Treat seed rhizomes with Copper Oxychloride before planting; spray Mancozeb for leaf spot.
best_production_tips: Boiling the harvested rhizomes within 2-3 days, followed by sun-drying for 10-15 days, is essential to develop the rich yellow color.
harvesting_stage: Harvest at 8-9 months when leaves and stems turn completely yellow and dry up.
harvesting_method: Dig out rhizomes carefully with a spade, cut the leaves, and clean soil.
post_harvest_handling: Sort mother rhizomes for seeds. Boil the remaining rhizomes in water for 45 mins, dry them in the sun, and polish them to remove rough skin.
expected_yield: 20 to 25 tons per hectare (1000 to 1250 kg per ropani) of fresh turmeric.
common_mistakes: Harvesting immature crops, which leads to poor curcumin content and low market price.
safety_note: Wear old clothes and gloves during boiling and slicing as turmeric stains are highly persistent.

suitable_climate: Warm climate, temperature between 20°C to 30°C. Frost is fatal. Heavy rain during flowering causes flower drop.
suitable_soil: Well-drained sandy loam or clay loam soils with high organic matter. pH of 6.0 to 7.0.
land_preparation: Plough 3 times, break clods, apply manure, and make ridges or raised beds.
seed_selection: Use high-yielding/hybrid seeds (e.g., Jwala, Akabare/Dalle chili in Nepal).
seed_rate: 1.0 to 1.5 kg per hectare for open-pollinated; 250g for hybrids (10-15 grams per ropani).
nursery_or_direct_sowing: Nursery sowing, transplant seedlings when 5-6 weeks old (15 cm high).
sowing_time: February to March for spring crop; September to October for winter crop.
planting_steps: Transplant healthy seedlings on ridges or raised beds in the late afternoon. Water immediately.
irrigation_schedule: Irrigate weekly during dry periods. Do not overwater as chili is sensitive to root rot. Keep soil moist during flowering.
manure_and_fertilizer: Apply 10-15 tons of compost. Recommended N:P:K is 100:60:60 kg/ha. Note: Exact quantity depends on soil test.
organic_fertilizer_option: Apply 15 tons of compost, neem cake, and spray liquid organic fertilizer (Jholmol) every 10 days.
chemical_fertilizer_option: Apply Urea (217 kg/ha), Single Super Phosphate (375 kg/ha), and Muriate of Potash (100 kg/ha).
fertilizer_schedule_by_stage: Basal: 50% nitrogen, 100% phosphorus, and 100% potassium at transplanting. Top dressings: 25% nitrogen at 30 days and 25% nitrogen at 60 days (flowering stage).
weed_management: Weeding and shallow hoeing 2-3 times during the first 60 days to keep the soil loose.
pest_management: Thrips, Aphids, and Fruit Borer. Spray neem extract or Imidacloprid for thrips/aphids. Use pheromone traps for fruit borer.
disease_management: Damping-off in nursery; Anthracnose/Dieback. Seed treatment with Thiram; spray Copper Oxychloride for Anthracnose.
best_production_tips: Provide staking for heavily-bearing hybrid chili varieties (like Akabare) to prevent branches from breaking.
harvesting_stage: Harvest green chilies for fresh market; let them turn red-ripe on the plant for dry chili production.
harvesting_method: Pluck chilies along with the stalk gently to avoid damaging the branches.
post_harvest_handling: Spread chilies in shade to remove surface moisture. Pack green chilies in ventilated bags. Dry red chilies in sun for 5-7 days.
expected_yield: 10 to 15 tons per hectare (500 to 750 kg per ropani) green chili; 1.5 to 2.5 tons/ha dry chili.
common_mistakes: Over-irrigating, which leads to flower drop and root rot. Keeping seedlings in nursery too long (>45 days) stunts growth.
safety_note: Wear gloves and do not touch eyes or face when harvesting or handling hot chilies (especially Akabare).

suitable_climate: Warm climate, temperature between 25°C to 35°C. Sensitive to frost. Requires bright sunlight and moderate humidity.
suitable_soil: Rich, well-drained sandy loam or loamy soils. pH of 6.0 to 7.0.
land_preparation: Plough 2-3 times. Prepare pits (size 45x45x45 cm) or long trenches with 1.5-2 meter spacing.
seed_selection: Use hybrid seeds (e.g., Bhaktapur Local, Dynasty, or Green Long in Nepal) for disease resistance.
seed_rate: 2.5 to 3.0 kg per hectare (150 grams per ropani).
nursery_or_direct_sowing: Direct sowing of seeds in pits.
sowing_time: February to March (spring crop); June to July (rainy crop).
spacing: 1.5 to 2.0 meters row-to-row and 60 cm plant-to-plant (or 3-4 seeds per pit).
planting_steps: Sow 3-4 seeds per pit at 2 cm depth. After germination, thin to retain 2 healthy plants per pit. Cover with mulch.
irrigation_schedule: Irrigate every 4-5 days during summer; every 10 days in winter. Never let soil dry completely during flowering/fruiting.
manure_and_fertilizer: Apply 15-20 tons of compost. Recommended N:P:K is 80:50:80 kg/ha. Note: Exact quantity depends on soil test.
organic_fertilizer_option: Apply 20 tons of compost, mustard cake (50g per pit), and wood ash.
chemical_fertilizer_option: Apply Urea (174 kg/ha), Single Super Phosphate (312 kg/ha), and Muriate of Potash (133 kg/ha).
fertilizer_schedule_by_stage: Basal: Apply 100% compost, 100% P, 50% K, and 33% N in pits before sowing. Top dressing: Apply 33% nitrogen at 30 days (vining stage) and remaining 34% nitrogen + 50% potassium at 50 days (flowering stage).
weed_management: Weeding and shallow hoeing around pits twice. Mulch around the base of vines.
pest_management: Red Pumpkin Beetle and Fruit Fly. Use pheromone traps (Pherolure) for fruit flies; handpick pumpkin beetles early in morning.
disease_management: Powdery Mildew and Downy Mildew. Spray Sulphur fungicides for Powdery Mildew; spray Metalaxyl for Downy Mildew.
best_production_tips: Grow on vertical trellises (bamboo staking and net) to keep cucumbers clean, straight, and prevent rot.
harvesting_stage: Harvest when fruits are young, tender, crisp, and green, before they turn yellow and seeds become hard.
harvesting_method: Cut fruits from the vine using a sharp knife or pruning shears with a short stem.
post_harvest_handling: Wipe with a clean damp cloth, sort by size, pack in plastic crates, and store in a cool place (10°C-12°C).
expected_yield: 15 to 25 tons per hectare (750 to 1250 kg per ropani).
common_mistakes: Allowing fruits to turn yellow on the vine, which stops the plant from producing new flowers and fruits.
safety_note: Handle climbing trellises carefully. Wash cucumbers thoroughly before eating to remove dirt and residues.

suitable_climate: Warm and hot climate, temperature between 25°C to 35°C. Highly tolerant to heat but sensitive to frost.
suitable_soil: Well-drained, sandy loam soil rich in organic matter. pH of 6.0 to 7.0.
land_preparation: Plough 2-3 times, prepare wide pits of 60x60x60 cm spaced 2.5 to 3 meters apart.
seed_selection: Choose local varieties or hybrids (e.g., Rampur Local, Kabuli).
seed_rate: 3.0 to 4.0 kg per hectare (200 grams per ropani).
nursery_or_direct_sowing: Direct sowing of seeds in pits.
sowing_time: January to March; June to July.
spacing: 2.5 to 3.0 meters row-to-row and 1.0 meter plant-to-plant (or 2 plants per pit).
planting_steps: Sow 4-5 seeds per pit at 3 cm depth. Thin out to 2 healthiest seedlings after 15 days.
irrigation_schedule: Irrigate every 7-10 days. Drought-tolerant once established, but requires water during flowering and fruit development.
manure_and_fertilizer: Apply 15-20 tons of compost. Recommended N:P:K is 80:60:80 kg/ha. Note: Exact quantity depends on soil test.
organic_fertilizer_option: Apply 10 kg of compost/FYM per pit, along with neem cake and ash.
chemical_fertilizer_option: Apply Urea (174 kg/ha), Single Super Phosphate (375 kg/ha), and Muriate of Potash (133 kg/ha).
fertilizer_schedule_by_stage: Basal: 100% compost, 100% P, and 50% K + 33% N in pits. Top dressing: 33% N at vining stage (30 days); 34% N + 50% K at flowering stage (50-60 days).
weed_management: Weeding around the pits during early growth. Once vines cover the ground, weeding is not necessary.
pest_management: Fruit Fly and Red Pumpkin Beetle. Use cue-lure pheromone traps for fruit flies.
disease_management: Powdery Mildew and Downy Mildew. Spray Copper Oxychloride or Mancozeb if mildew spots appear.
best_production_tips: Place a flat stone or dry straw under developing pumpkins to prevent contact with wet soil and avoid rotting.
harvesting_stage: Harvest when fruits turn yellow-orange, the skin becomes hard, and the stalk turns woody and dry.
harvesting_method: Cut the fruit from the vine leaving 5 cm of stem/stalk attached to improve shelf-life.
post_harvest_handling: Clean fruits, cure in sun for 3-4 days to harden skin. Store in a well-ventilated dry room. Can store for 3-6 months.
expected_yield: 20 to 30 tons per hectare (1000 to 1500 kg per ropani).
common_mistakes: Cutting the stem too short during harvest, which allows pathogens to enter and causes rot during storage.
safety_note: Keep chemicals away from the edible fruit. Store pumpkins in dry conditions to avoid mold.

suitable_climate: Cool and dry climate during growth. Temperature of 15°C to 25°C. Requires clear sunny days and is sensitive to frost.
suitable_soil: Alluvial, sandy loam loam soils with good drainage. pH of 6.0 to 7.5.
land_preparation: Ploughing 3 times, followed by harrowing and leveling to make a fine, moist seedbed.
seed_selection: Use high-yielding, high oil content certified seeds (e.g., Preeti, Pragati in Nepal).
seed_rate: 6 to 8 kg per hectare (300-400 grams per ropani).
nursery_or_direct_sowing: Direct sowing. Usually broadcasted or sown in lines.
sowing_time: October to November (mostly mid-October).
spacing: 30 cm row-to-row and 10 cm plant-to-plant (thinning is required).
planting_steps: Sow seeds in lines at a shallow depth of 2-3 cm. Light planking should follow to cover the seeds.
irrigation_schedule: Requires 2 critical irrigations: first at pre-flowering stage (30 days) and second at pod filling stage (60 days).
manure_and_fertilizer: Apply 6-8 tons of compost. Recommended N:P:K is 60:40:40 kg/ha. Sulphur is critical (20 kg/ha). Note: Exact quantity depends on soil test.
organic_fertilizer_option: Apply 8 tons of compost and gypsum (source of calcium and sulphur) at land preparation.
chemical_fertilizer_option: Apply Urea (130 kg/ha), Single Super Phosphate (250 kg/ha), Muriate of Potash (67 kg/ha), and Gypsum (100 kg/ha).
fertilizer_schedule_by_stage: Basal: Apply 50% nitrogen, 100% phosphorus, 100% potassium, and 100% sulphur at sowing. Top dressing: Apply remaining 50% nitrogen at first irrigation (30 days).
weed_management: Interculture and thinning at 20-25 days after sowing to maintain 10 cm distance between plants.
pest_management: Mustard Aphid is the major pest. Spray Neem oil or biological extracts. In severe cases, spray Dimethoate.
disease_management: Alternaria Leaf Spot and White Rust. Treat seeds with Metalaxyl; spray Mancozeb if disease occurs.
best_production_tips: Do not skip Sulphur application; it directly increases the oil content and yield of mustard seeds by 15-20%.
harvesting_stage: Harvest when 75% of the pods (silique) turn golden yellow and seeds inside turn brown/black.
harvesting_method: Cut the plants near the ground using a sickle. Bundle and dry in the sun for 4-5 days.
post_harvest_handling: Thresh by beating bundles on clean sheets. Winnow to separate seeds, dry seeds to 8% moisture content for safe oil extraction.
expected_yield: 1.2 to 1.8 tons per hectare (60 to 90 kg per ropani).
common_mistakes: Overcrowding of plants due to skipping the thinning process. This leads to thin stems and heavy aphid infestation.
safety_note: Wear protective gear when spraying insecticides for aphids. Avoid drift to nearby water bodies.

suitable_climate: Cool winter crop. Growing temperature of 15°C to 20°C. Tolerant to drought but highly sensitive to waterlogging.
suitable_soil: Well-drained loam or sandy loam soils. Tolerates slightly alkaline soils. pH of 6.0 to 8.0.
land_preparation: Ploughing 2-3 times followed by planking to get a level, weed-free seedbed.
seed_selection: Use certified varieties (e.g., Simal, Khajura-2, or Shikhar in Nepal). Treat seeds with Rhizobium culture.
seed_rate: 30 to 40 kg per hectare (1.5 to 2.0 kg per ropani).
nursery_or_direct_sowing: Direct sowing. Broadcast or line sowing.
sowing_time: October to November.
spacing: 25 cm row-to-row spacing, continuous planting in rows.
planting_steps: Sow seeds at a depth of 3-4 cm. Ensure inoculating seeds with Rhizobium before sowing to boost nitrogen fixation.
irrigation_schedule: Generally grown as a rainfed crop. If dry, irrigate once at flowering (40-45 days) and once at pod-fill (70-75 days).
manure_and_fertilizer: Apply 4-6 tons of compost. Recommended N:P:K is 20:40:20 kg/ha. (Needs low nitrogen due to nitrogen fixation). Note: Exact quantity depends on soil test.
organic_fertilizer_option: Apply 5 tons of compost, rock phosphate, and Rhizobium culture inoculant.
chemical_fertilizer_option: Apply Urea (43 kg/ha), Single Super Phosphate (250 kg/ha), and Muriate of Potash (33 kg/ha).
fertilizer_schedule_by_stage: Basal: Apply 100% of nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium at the time of sowing in rows.
weed_management: Hand weeding at 30 days after sowing. Lentil is a slow grower initially and requires weeding to prevent weed suppression.
pest_management: Pod Borer and Aphids. Spray Neem oil or Spinosad for pod borer.
disease_management: Wilt and Root Rot. Avoid waterlogging. Treat seeds with Trichoderma viride or Carbendazim.
best_production_tips: Rhizobium inoculation is cheap and increases lentil yield by 15-20% while fixing nitrogen for the next crop.
harvesting_stage: Harvest when pods turn golden brown, leaves turn yellow and begin to shed, and seeds are hard.
harvesting_method: Cut plants close to the ground or pull out manually. Dry in the sun for 3-4 days.
post_harvest_handling: Thresh dried plants, winnow to clean, and dry seeds to 10-12% moisture content before storage.
expected_yield: 1.0 to 1.5 tons per hectare (50 to 75 kg per ropani).
common_mistakes: Over-watering which causes immediate root rot and wilt. Over-applying nitrogen fertilizers (stunts root nodules).
safety_note: Use seed treatment chemicals safely. Wear gloves during seed inoculation.

suitable_climate: Warm and moist summer climate, temperature between 25°C to 30°C. Requires moderate rainfall during flowering.
suitable_soil: Well-drained alluvial, loam, or clay loam soils. Sensitive to waterlogging. pH of 6.0 to 7.5.
land_preparation: Ploughing 2-3 times followed by planking to create a level, clod-free seedbed.
seed_selection: Use high oil and protein varieties (e.g., Puja, Hardee, Kabindra in Nepal). Inoculate seeds with Bradyrhizobium.
seed_rate: 70 to 80 kg per hectare (3.5 to 4.0 kg per ropani).
nursery_or_direct_sowing: Direct sowing in lines.
sowing_time: June to July (monsoon).
planting_steps: Sow seeds at a depth of 3 cm. Treat seeds with Rhizobium culture and dry in shade before planting.
irrigation_schedule: Mainly grown as rainfed. If drought occurs, irrigate during flowering and pod development stages.
manure_and_fertilizer: Apply 6-8 tons of compost. Recommended N:P:K is 20:60:40 kg/ha. Note: Exact quantity depends on soil test.
organic_fertilizer_option: Apply 8 tons of compost, rock phosphate, ash, and inoculate with Bradyrhizobium culture.
chemical_fertilizer_option: Apply Urea (43 kg/ha), Single Super Phosphate (375 kg/ha), and Muriate of Potash (67 kg/ha).
fertilizer_schedule_by_stage: Basal: Apply 100% of nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium at the time of sowing.
weed_management: Keep field weed-free for first 45 days. Weeding twice at 20 and 40 days.
pest_management: Hairy Caterpillar and Girdle Beetle. Handpick caterpillars in early stages; spray Neem oil.
disease_management: Rust and Yellow Mosaic Virus. Control whiteflies to prevent Mosaic. Use disease-free seeds.
best_production_tips: Avoid planting soybean seeds deeper than 4 cm, as it drastically reduces seedling emergence.
harvesting_stage: Harvest when leaves turn yellow and drop off, and pods turn brown and dry.
harvesting_method: Cut the plants close to the ground. Do not pull them out so that nitrogen-rich roots remain in the soil.
post_harvest_handling: Dry plants in sun for 3 days, thresh, winnow, and dry grains to 10% moisture content before storing in dry containers.
expected_yield: 1.5 to 2.5 tons per hectare (75 to 125 kg per ropani).
common_mistakes: planting poor viability seeds (soybean seeds lose viability quickly). Planting too deep.
safety_note: Bradyrhizobium inoculants are safe but avoid direct inhalation. Wear masks during chemical seed dressing.

suitable_climate: Tropical and subtropical climates. Temperatures between 20°C to 30°C. Needs high humidity and abundant water. Highly sensitive to strong winds.
suitable_soil: Deep, rich, well-drained loamy or alluvial soil. pH of 6.0 to 7.5.
land_preparation: Dig pits of size 60x60x60 cm spaced 1.8 to 2 meters apart.
seed_selection: Use healthy, disease-free sword suckers weighing 1.5 to 2.0 kg, or tissue culture plants (e.g., William Hybrid, G-9, Harichhal in Nepal).
seed_rate: 2500 to 3000 suckers per hectare (120-150 per ropani).
nursery_or_direct_sowing: Direct planting of suckers or tissue culture plants in pits.
sowing_time: June to August; February to March.
spacing: 1.8 meters by 1.8 meters or 2.0 meters by 2.0 meters.
planting_steps: Fill pits with 10 kg compost and topsoil. Plant sucker vertically in the center, pack soil firmly around it, and irrigate immediately.
irrigation_schedule: Requires heavy watering. Irrigate every 4-5 days in summer; every 10-15 days in winter. Maintain soil moisture.
manure_and_fertilizer: Heavy feeder. Apply 10-15 kg compost per plant. Recommended N:P:K is 250:100:300 grams per plant per year. Note: Exact quantity depends on soil test.
organic_fertilizer_option: Apply 15 kg compost, 1 kg neem cake, and 2 kg wood ash per plant. Apply organic liquid fertilizer monthly.
chemical_fertilizer_option: Apply Urea (540g/plant), Single Super Phosphate (625g/plant), and Muriate of Potash (500g/plant) per year.
fertilizer_schedule_by_stage: Split doses: Phosphorus is applied basal. Nitrogen and Potassium are applied in 4 equal split doses at 2, 4, 6, and 8 months after planting.
weed_management: Keep the basin weed-free. Mulch around the plant base with banana leaves or straw to conserve water and suppress weeds.
pest_management: Banana Weevil and Aphids. Apply neem cake in the pit. Inject pseudostem with neem extract.
disease_management: Panama Wilt and Sigatoka Leaf Spot. Avoid waterlogging. Drench soil with Carbendazim for Panama Wilt; spray Copper fungicides for Sigatoka.
best_production_tips: Desuckering (removing unwanted side suckers, leaving only one main plant and one follower) is crucial for large banana bunches.
harvesting_stage: Harvest 11-13 months after planting, when fruit ridges turn round, flowers dry up, and fruits turn light green.
harvesting_method: Cut the bunch leaving 30 cm of stalk. Handle carefully to avoid bruising.
post_harvest_handling: Wash bunch with water to remove latex, wrap in newspaper or foam sheets, pack in crates, and keep in shade.
expected_yield: 30 to 45 tons per hectare (1500 to 2250 kg per ropani).
common_mistakes: Allowing too many suckers to grow around the mother plant, which reduces the size and weight of the main banana bunch.
safety_note: Take care when cutting tall pseudostems during harvest. Wear old clothing to avoid permanent stains from banana sap.

suitable_climate: Tropical and subtropical climates. Requires warm, dry weather during flowering and fruit ripening. Sensitive to frost.
suitable_soil: Deep, fertile, well-drained loamy soils. Can grow in varied soils. pH of 5.5 to 7.5.
land_preparation: Dig pits of size 1x1x1 meter spaced 10 meters apart (or 5-6 meters for high density).
seed_selection: Use grafted plants of high-quality varieties (e.g., Alphonso, Amrapali, Dashehari, Maldaha in Nepal).
seed_rate: 100 plants per hectare (5 per ropani) for standard spacing.
nursery_or_direct_sowing: Direct planting of grafted saplings in pits.
sowing_time: July to August (monsoon season).
spacing: 10 meters by 10 meters (standard) or 5 meters by 5 meters (high density).
planting_steps: Fill pits with topsoil, 20 kg compost, and 1 kg bone meal. Plant sapling graft-union 15 cm above ground level. Stake and water.
irrigation_schedule: Water newly planted saplings every 2-3 days. For mature trees, irrigate every 10-15 days during fruit development. Stop watering 2 months before flowering.
manure_and_fertilizer: Apply 20-30 kg compost per tree. Recommended N:P:K for mature tree (10+ years) is 1.0:0.5:1.0 kg per tree per year. Note: Exact quantity depends on soil test.
organic_fertilizer_option: Apply 30-40 kg compost, 2 kg neem cake, and 3 kg wood ash per tree in a ring around the drip line.
chemical_fertilizer_option: Apply Urea (2.2 kg), Single Super Phosphate (3.1 kg), and Muriate of Potash (1.7 kg) per mature tree per year.
fertilizer_schedule_by_stage: Apply in two split doses: 50% after harvest (June-July) along with compost; 50% during October (before flowering).
weed_management: Keep the area under the tree canopy clean. Grow cover crops (like cowpea) in the interspaces during early years.
pest_management: Mango Hopper and Mealy Bug. Spray neem-based sprays during flowering. Apply grease bands on trunks to block crawling mealy bugs.
disease_management: Powdery Mildew and Anthracnose. Spray Wettable Sulphur for Powdery Mildew; spray Carbendazim for Anthracnose.
best_production_tips: Prune dead, diseased, and overlapping branches after harvest to allow sunlight into the center of the canopy, promoting flowering.
harvesting_stage: Harvest when fruits turn pale green/yellowish, and latex flow is low when plucked (usually 4-5 months after flowering).
harvesting_method: Pluck fruits with a stem stalk of 1-2 cm using a mango picker (sickle with net) to prevent latex drip on skin.
post_harvest_handling: Desap harvested mangoes (place stem down on racks to drain sap for 4 hours). Wash, grade by size, pack in boxes, and store at 12°C.
expected_yield: 10 to 15 tons per hectare (500 to 750 kg per ropani) for mature orchards.
common_mistakes: Watering trees during flowering, which causes flowers to drop and stimulates vegetative leaf growth instead of fruits.
safety_note: Mango sap contains urushiol-like compounds that can cause skin rashes. Wash skin immediately if contact occurs.

suitable_climate: Subtropical climate, ideal temperature between 13°C to 30°C. Grows best in hills (altitude 800m to 1400m). Needs clear sun.
suitable_soil: Deep, well-drained, sandy loam or loamy soils rich in organic matter. Avoid clay. pH of 5.5 to 6.5.
land_preparation: Dig pits of size 75x75x75 cm spaced 5 to 6 meters apart. Ensure orchard is on terraces if on a hill slope.
seed_selection: Use healthy, disease-free grafted or budded saplings (e.g., Suntala, Local Mandarin in Nepal) on rootstocks like trifoliate orange.
seed_rate: 270 to 400 plants per hectare (15-20 per ropani).
nursery_or_direct_sowing: Direct planting of grafted saplings in pits.
sowing_time: June to July (onset of monsoon).
spacing: 5 meters by 5 meters or 6 meters by 6 meters.
planting_steps: Fill pits with topsoil, 15 kg compost, and 500g bone meal. Plant sapling. Ensure graft union is well above soil. Water and mulch.
irrigation_schedule: Water weekly for young plants. Irrigate mature trees every 10-15 days during fruit growth. Avoid irrigation during flower bud induction.
manure_and_fertilizer: Apply 15-20 kg compost per tree. Recommended N:P:K for mature tree (8+ years) is 600:300:600 grams per tree per year. Note: Exact quantity depends on soil test.
organic_fertilizer_option: Apply 25 kg compost, 1 kg bone meal, 1.5 kg wood ash, and biofertilizers in a ring 1 meter away from the trunk.
chemical_fertilizer_option: Apply Urea (1.3 kg), Single Super Phosphate (1.8 kg), and Muriate of Potash (1.0 kg) per mature tree per year.
fertilizer_schedule_by_stage: Apply in three split doses: 33% nitrogen + 100% phosphorus + 50% potassium in February (before flowering); 33% nitrogen + 50% potassium in June; remaining 34% nitrogen in September.
weed_management: Keep the basin weed-free. Mulch basins with dry grass. Keep orchard interspaces clean or grow legumes.
pest_management: Citrus Psylla, Leaf Miner, and Lemon Butterfly. Spray neem oil for leaf miners; use biological control or spray Imidacloprid for citrus psylla.
disease_management: Citrus Decline (Greening) and Gummosis. Avoid water logging to prevent Gummosis; graft on resistant rootstock. Remove infected decline trees.
best_production_tips: Do not allow weeds or water to touch the tree trunk directly; this prevents Gummosis (bark rot and sap oozing).
harvesting_stage: Harvest when fruits turn fully orange-yellow and sugar-to-acid ratio is sweet.
harvesting_method: Harvest by clipping the fruit stalk with a sharp clipper. Do not pull fruits as it tears the skin.
post_harvest_handling: Sort and discard damaged fruits. Wash, cure in shade for 2 days, wrap in paper, pack in crates, and store in a cool, well-ventilated room.
expected_yield: 12 to 18 tons per hectare (600 to 900 kg per ropani) for a mature orchard.
common_mistakes: Over-watering the orchard during winter, which leads to flower drop and root rot. Pulling fruits instead of clipping.
safety_note: Observe pre-harvest intervals after spraying. Wash clippers before and after pruning to prevent disease spread.

suitable_climate: Cold temperate climate. Requires 1000-1500 chilling hours below 7°C. Grows best at altitudes above 1800m (e.g., Mustang, Jumla in Nepal).
suitable_soil: Deep, rich, well-drained loamy soils. Sensitive to waterlogging. pH of 6.0 to 6.8.
land_preparation: Dig pits of size 90x90x90 cm spaced 4 to 6 meters apart. Ensure proper terrace layout in hills.
seed_selection: Use grafted cultivars (e.g., Royal Delicious, Golden Delicious, Fuji in Nepal) on clonal rootstocks like M9 or MM106.
seed_rate: 400 to 600 plants per hectare (20-30 per ropani).
nursery_or_direct_sowing: Direct planting of grafted dormant bare-root saplings.
sowing_time: January to February (dormant season).
spacing: 4 meters by 4 meters or 5 meters by 5 meters.
planting_steps: Fill pits with topsoil, 20 kg compost, and 500g superphosphate. Plant bare-root sapling, keep graft union 10 cm above soil level. Stake and water.
irrigation_schedule: Irrigate at critical stages: bud break, flowering, fruit set, and fruit growth. Stop watering before leaf fall in winter.
manure_and_fertilizer: Apply 20-30 kg compost per tree. Recommended N:P:K for mature tree (10+ years) is 500:250:500 grams per tree per year. Note: Exact quantity depends on soil test.
organic_fertilizer_option: Apply 30 kg compost, 1 kg bone meal, 2 kg wood ash, and organic mulch. Apply biofertilizers in spring.
chemical_fertilizer_option: Apply Urea (1.1 kg), Single Super Phosphate (1.5 kg), and Muriate of Potash (830g) per mature tree per year.
fertilizer_schedule_by_stage: Apply compost, phosphorus, and potassium in December-January (dormant stage). Apply nitrogen in two split doses: 50% in spring (bud break) and 50% in June (fruit expansion).
weed_management: Keep tree basins clean and heavily mulched. Mulching with straw or black plastic helps suppress weeds and retain soil moisture.
pest_management: Woolly Apple Aphid and San Jose Scale. Spray horticultural mineral oil during dormant phase. Use predatory wasps.
disease_management: Apple Scab and Powdery Mildew. Spray Copper Oxychloride before bud break; spray Carbendazim or Mancozeb for Apple Scab control.
best_production_tips: Winter pruning is vital to train the tree (central leader system), allowing sunlight to reach all parts of the canopy for quality fruits.
harvesting_stage: Harvest when fruits develop full color, seeds inside turn dark brown, and flesh is sweet and crisp.
harvesting_method: Pluck manually with an upward twisting motion so the fruit stem remains intact on the fruit.
post_harvest_handling: Handle gently. Sort, grade, wrap in tissue paper, pack in boxes, and store immediately in cold storage (0°C-2°C) or cool cellars.
expected_yield: 10 to 20 tons per hectare (500 to 1000 kg per ropani) depending on variety and density.
common_mistakes: Neglecting winter pruning, leading to crowded branches, small fruits, and high scab incidence. Harvesting early.
safety_note: Handle clippers and pruning saws with care. Disinfect tools with alcohol to avoid spreading fire blight.
